blurb: The Centre for the Governance of AI (GovAI), an Oxford-rooted research nonprofit producing policy research on how to govern advanced/frontier AI.

hiring.note: Official GovAI opportunities page checked 2026-07-02; current London AI-governance fellowships are first-party verified.

publications.title: Frontier AI Auditing: Toward Rigorous Third-Party Assessment of Safety and Security Practices at Leading AI Companies
publications.byline: Miles Brundage, Noemi Dreksler, Aidan Homewood, Sean McGregor, Patricia Paskov, Conrad Stosz, Girish Sastry, A. Feder Cooper, George Balston, Steven Adler, Stephen Casper, Markus Anderljung et al.
publications.summary: Outlines a vision for frontier AI auditing as rigorous third-party verification of developers' safety and security claims based on deep, secure access to non-public information (not just deployed products). Proposes four AI Assurance Levels (AALs), recommending AAL-1 as a baseline for frontier AI and AAL-2 as a near-term goal for the most advanced developers. arXiv:2601.11699.

publications.title: Does the UAE Have an Advantage in Building Data Centers?
publications.summary: Examines whether the UAE holds structural advantages (energy, capital, siting) for large-scale AI data-center buildout and what that means for compute geopolitics.

publications.title: Dual-Use AI Capabilities and the Risk of Bioterrorism: Converting Capability Evaluations to Risk Assessments
publications.summary: Proposes a method for translating model capability evaluations into actual bioterrorism risk assessments rather than treating capability as risk directly.

publications.title: How Adaptable Are American Workers to AI-Induced Job Displacement?
publications.summary: Studies the adaptability of US workers to AI-driven displacement and the labour-market implications for policy.

roles.title: Summer/Winter Fellowship (AI governance & public policy, Research Track)
